title: Bitter Velvety Turkish Dried-shrimp-goong-haeng Brunch
description: A rich, savory brunch dish featuring dried shrimp-goong-haeng and low-glycemic ingredients, with a hint of bitterness from the cured elements.
instructions: Combine the dried goong-haeng shrimp, cured goong-haeng, and low-glycemic white rice in a bowl, breaking up any lumps with a fork.
Add the extra-virgin olive oil, fresh parsley, fresh dill, low-sodium soy sauce, and freshly squeezed lemon juice to the bowl, stirring to combine.
Season the mixture with ground black pepper, garlic powder, cured black pepper, salt, and Turkish red pepper flakes, stirring to combine.

Heat a large skillet over medium heat, with a small amount of oil to prevent sticking, and sauté the goong-haeng mixture for 3-4 minutes, stirring frequently, until it reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
Serve the Bitter Velvety Turkish Dried-shrimp-goong-haeng Brunch hot, garnished with fresh parsley and lemon wedges, if desired.
Let the dish rest for a few minutes before serving.
Use a food thermometer to ensure the dish has reached a safe internal temperature.
Serve with a dollop of low-glycemic yogurt, if desired.
